subject: Geography
title: The Rise of Megacities
description: A fast breakdown of The Rise of Megacities for builders.

hook.text: A megacity has over 10 million people. In 1950, only two existed. Today there are over 30.

buildup.text: Tokyo, New York, and Mexico City were the first to cross the threshold.

discovery.text: By 2030, the UN projects 43 megacities, most of them in Asia and Africa.

twist.text: For the first time in history, more people live in cities than in rural areas.

climax.text: Megacities are the new organizing unit of human civilization, reshaping economics and culture.

punchline.text: Humanity's future is urban. The megacity era has begun.
